relied_claims: System B produced a narrowed risk-routing summary.
System A source-backed triage context existed upstream.
preserved_non_claims: System B did not establish approval authority for System C.
System A did not transfer approval authority.
The routed summary did not determine compliance.
The routed summary did not certify correctness.
attempted_downstream_use: Treat the narrowed routing summary as sufficient basis for approval-routing authority.
expansion_reason: System C attempted to rely on a routing summary as if authority to approve had been inherited through the distributed handoff.
unresolved_unknowns: whether the delegated approval authority exists
whether policy criteria have been satisfied
required_next_action: Obtain and bind a separate approval-authority claim boundary contract before any downstream approval or execution reliance.
fork_non_authority_statement: Fork records the expanded consumption event and required revalidation. It does not decide whether the downstream decision is acceptable.
